raw_consumer_statement: I contracted with Home Depot for a cabinet refacing job on XXXX XXXX. The job began on XX/XX/year>. It was supposed to take 2-5 days for completion. On XX/XX/year>, day five, the contractor ended the job but the refacing job was not completed. There were numerous items missing, such as all moldings, all cabinet handles/hardware, and two drawers. Additionally, the laminate was not installed properly causing delamination in areas, as well as uneven cuts, cracks, holes and scratches. The cabinet doors were the wrong size and all the drawers were installed unevenly and/or were warped. The soft close guides were installed unevenly and some were missing screws. Home Depot sent their own investigator out to corroborate the problems. Since the installer left the job in an incomplete and deficient state, and was unable to remedy the problems due to his own admission that he was finished with the job and there was nothing further he could do, and given that Home Depot was paid in full via my Home Depot credit card ( {$19000.00} ) at the time of the contract signing ( XX/XX/year> ), I did a charge back with the credit card issuer, Citibank. The charge back was initially approved but then reversed due to Home Depot disputing this action. I did not/have not received the installation services I purchased yet I am still paying my Citibank credit card for an item not received.
